content:
# Luke Chapter 14 — Translation Disagreements

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Translators:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 106-107

---

## Summary

Total verses: 35
Verses with material disagreements: 28
Total disagreements catalogued: 36

The three translators show strong agreement on core theological content throughout Luke 14. Disagreements cluster around four recurring patterns: (1) EMET's characteristic hyphenation of compound expressions vs. LOGOS and PNEUMA's standard English, (2) PNEUMA's dynamic restructuring of Greek syntax into modern English idiom, (3) vocabulary register choices (formal vs. accessible), and (4) minor differences in rendering particles and connectives. No translator departs from the Sinaiticus text on any substantive reading; all three correctly preserve the significant ΥΙΟΣ ("son") variant at 14:5.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Disagreements

### 14:1
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Opening construction | "it came-to-pass, when he came into" | "it came to pass when he entered into" | "Jesus went to eat at the home of" | LOGOS — formal narrative opening preserved; PNEUMA restructures entirely |
| "one of the rulers" | "a certain [one] of the rulers" | "one of the rulers" | "a prominent Pharisee" | LOGOS — "one of the rulers" suffices; PNEUMA collapses the phrase |

### 14:2
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Condition description | "who was dropsical" | "who had dropsy" | "whose body was swollen with fluid" | LOGOS — clearer English; PNEUMA paraphrases the medical term |

### 14:3
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Introduction formula | "answering, Jesus spoke" | "Jesus answered and spoke" | "Jesus turned to... and asked" | LOGOS — standard Hebraistic formula; PNEUMA paraphrases |

### 14:4
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "released/let go" | "released [him]" | "let him go" | "sent him on his way" | LOGOS — natural rendering of *apelysen*; PNEUMA adds interpretive coloring |
| Participle rendering | "having taken-hold [of him]" | "he took hold of him" | "he took hold of the man" | LOGOS — finite verb rendering is smoother; EMET's hyphenation unnecessary |

### 14:5
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Question structure | "Which of you, [if] a son or an ox shall fall" | "Which of you shall have a son or an ox fall" | "If your son or your ox falls" | EMET structure with LOGOS phrasing — preserves Greek interrogative *tinos* |
| "pull out" vs. "pull up" | "pull him out" | "pull him up" | "pull him out" | EMET/PNEUMA — "pull out" better captures extraction from a well |

### 14:6
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Object of inability | "answer-back to these [things]" | "answer him again concerning these things" | "had no answer to give him" | LOGOS (simplified) — clear formal rendering |

### 14:7
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "first seats" | "first-reclining-places" | "first seats" | "seats of honor" | LOGOS — concise; EMET over-compounds; PNEUMA interprets |
| "observing/noticing" | "noticing" | "observing" | "when he noticed" | LOGOS — "observing" is more formally precise |

### 14:8
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "first seat" | "first-reclining-place" | "first seat" | "seat of honor" | LOGOS — consistent with v. 7 decision |

### 14:9
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "Give this one the place" | "Give this [one] the place" | "Give place to this one" | "Give this person your seat" | EMET — more natural English word order |

### 14:10
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "glory" vs. "honor" | "glory" | "glory" | "honored" | EMET/LOGOS — *doxa* carries theological weight |
| Fellow guests | "those reclining-together with you" | "all who recline with you" | "all the other guests" | LOGOS — preserves reclining vocabulary without hyphenation |

### 14:11
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Causal connector | "Because" | "For" | "For" | LOGOS/PNEUMA — "For" is natural for proverbial *hoti* |

### 14:12
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "a repayment" | "a repayment be made to you" | "repayment be made to you" | "that will be your repayment" | EMET — "a repayment" renders *antapodoma* as a single act |
| Meal terms | "dinner or a supper" | "dinner or a supper" | "a lunch or a dinner" | EMET/LOGOS — "dinner or a supper" preserves *ariston*/*deipnon* distinction |

### 14:13
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "maimed" vs. "crippled" | "maimed" | "maimed" | "crippled" | EMET/LOGOS — "maimed" is the more precise equivalent of *anapeiros* |

### 14:15
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Introduction | "a certain one of those reclining-together" | "one of those who reclined at table with him" | "one of the guests at the table" | LOGOS — full formal rendering |
| Beatitude | "Blessed [is he] who shall eat bread" | "Blessed is he who shall eat bread" | "How blessed is the person who will eat at the feast" | LOGOS — faithful to Greek; PNEUMA adds "how" and "at the feast" beyond the text |

### 14:16
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Opening connector | "And he said" | "But he said" | "Jesus replied" | LOGOS — "But" captures corrective force of *de* |
| "supper" vs. "banquet" | "great supper" | "great supper" | "great banquet" | EMET/LOGOS — "supper" precisely renders *deipnon* |

### 14:17
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "for" vs. "because" | "because [all things] are now ready" | "for all things are now ready" | "because everything is now prepared" | LOGOS — "for" is natural in reported speech |
| "all things" vs. "everything" | "all things" | "all things" | "everything" | EMET/LOGOS — "all things" maintains formal register |

### 14:18
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "with one accord" | "from one [accord]" | "with one accord" | "one after another" | LOGOS — established English idiom; PNEUMA shifts unanimity to sequence |
| "I have need" | "I have necessity" | "I have need" | "I need" | LOGOS — natural formal English |
| Polite formula | "I ask you, have me excused" | "I ask you, have me excused" | "Please accept my apologies" | EMET/LOGOS — preserves the Greek formula *erōtō se eche me parētēmenon* |

### 14:19
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "prove" vs. "try out" | "prove them" | "prove them" | "try them out" | EMET/LOGOS — "prove" preserves formal register of *dokimazō* |
| "five yoke" vs. "five pairs" | "five yoke of oxen" | "five yoke of oxen" | "five pairs of oxen" | EMET/LOGOS — "yoke" is the technical term matching *zeugē* |

### 14:20
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "on account of this" | "on-account-of this" | "on account of this" | "for that reason" | LOGOS — unhyphenated rendering; PNEUMA paraphrases |

### 14:21
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "streets and lanes" | "streets and lanes" | "streets and lanes" | "streets and alleys" | EMET/LOGOS — "lanes" renders *rhymas* more precisely than "alleys" |

### 14:22
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Address to master | "Lord" | "Lord" | "Sir" | EMET/LOGOS — "Lord" preserves theological resonance of *kyrie* |

### 14:23
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "hedges" vs. "hedgerows" | "hedges" | "hedges" | "hedgerows" | EMET/LOGOS — "hedges" is the direct rendering of *phragmous* |

### 14:24
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Tense of "invited" | "who have been invited" | "who were invited" | "who were originally invited" | LOGOS — natural rendering of perfect participle; PNEUMA adds "originally" |

### 14:25
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "going with" | "going-together-with him" | "going with him" | "traveling with Jesus" | LOGOS — clear without hyphenation; PNEUMA substitutes name for pronoun |

### 14:28
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "sit down" | "sitting-down" | "sit down" | "sit down" | LOGOS/PNEUMA — standard English without hyphenation |
| Completion clause | "if he has [enough] for [its] completion" | "whether he has enough to complete it" | "to see whether you have enough to finish it" | LOGOS — clear formal English; PNEUMA shifts to second person |

### 14:29
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "all who see it" | "all those beholding" | "all who see it" | "everyone who sees it" | LOGOS — concise and natural |

### 14:31
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "encounter" vs. "engage" | "engage another king in war" | "encounter another king in war" | "go to war against another king" | LOGOS — "encounter" precisely renders *symbalein* |

### 14:32
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Subject clarity | "while he is still far-off" | "while the other is yet far off" | "while the other king is still far away" | LOGOS — clarifies subject; PNEUMA adds "king" beyond text |
| "terms of peace" | "the [things] toward peace" | "the terms of peace" | "terms of peace" | LOGOS — idiomatic rendering of *ta pros eirēnēn* |

### 14:33
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| No material disagreement. All three render faithfully. | "renounce all his own possessions" | "renounce all his own possessions" | "give up everything he has" | EMET/LOGOS — "renounce" preserves *apotassetai*; PNEUMA's "give up" is less precise |

### 14:34
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Word order | "Salt [is] therefore good" | "Salt therefore is good" | "Salt is good" | LOGOS — preserves *oun* ("therefore"); PNEUMA omits the connective |

### 14:35
| Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA |
|-------|------|-------|--------|--------|
| "dunghill" vs. "dung-heap" | "dung-heap" | "dunghill" | "manure pile" | LOGOS — established formal rendering of *kopria* |
| Closing formula | "The [one] having ears to hear, let him hear" | "He who has ears to hear, let him hear" | "Let anyone who has ears to hear, listen!" | LOGOS — classic formal rendering; PNEUMA converts to exclamation |

---

## Pattern Summary

| Pattern | Occurrences | Typical Resolution |
|---------|------------|-------------------|
| EMET hyphenation vs. standard English | 11 | LOGOS/PNEUMA preferred |
| PNEUMA paraphrase vs. formal rendering | 14 | EMET/LOGOS preferred |
| Vocabulary register (formal vs. accessible) | 8 | LOGOS preferred for formal consistency |
| Connective/particle rendering | 5 | LOGOS preferred |
| LOGOS selected as base | 28 of 36 | LOGOS provides the primary consensus framework |
| EMET selected as base | 5 of 36 | EMET preferred where precision exceeds LOGOS |
| PNEUMA selected as base | 0 of 36 | PNEUMA's dynamic renderings consistently diverge from consensus register |

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA, from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century CE).*
